title: pseudixus japonicus
intro: Pseudixus japonicus, also known as Japanese angelica or yomogi, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pseudixus japonicus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant are often consumed as tea, capsules, or added to soups and infusions, and it is also used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
